zh.Rankilburn, Crosslee, Teviotdale, Scotla: 英國蘇格蘭雷福福爾可洛素利
zh.lifeSummary: Richard Scott 1st Of Rankilburn and Murthuckston是William Scott Balliol和Unknown Scott的兒子，於1275年出生在英國蘇格蘭。他和Inglis Murthuckstone至少有1個兒子。他去世於1320年享年45歲。

nameMeanings.name: Scott
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (Down): habitational and ethnic name from Middle English Scot ‘man from Scotland’. There is no evidence that the surname denoted either of the earlier senses of Scot as ‘(Gaelic-speaking) Irishman’ or ‘man from Alba’, the Gaelic-speaking region of Scotland north of the river Forth. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English and Scottish: from the rare Middle English personal name Scot (Old English Scott, possibly also Old Norse Skotr), only certainly attested in northern England.

variants.name: Scutt
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Pierre.
English: nickname for a short person, from Middle English scut(e) ‘short’, or else for a swift runner, from Middle English scut ‘hare’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: Richard Scott 1st Of Rankilburn and Murthuckston was born in 1275, in Scotland, United Kingdom as the son of William Scott Balliol and Unknown Scott. He had at least 1 son with Inglis Murthuckstone. He died in 1320, at the age of 45.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard Scott 1st Of Rankilburn and Murthuckston est né en 1275, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni, fils de William Scott Balliol et Unknown Scott. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Inglis Murthuckstone. Il est décédé en 1320, à l’âge de 45 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Richard Scott 1st Of Rankilburn and Murthuckston wurde 1275 in Schottland, United Kingdom, als Sohn von William Scott Balliol und Unknown Scott geboren. Er hatte mit Inglis Murthuckstone mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1320 im Alter von 45 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Richard Scott 1st Of Rankilburn and Murthuckston è nato il nel 1275 nel Scozia, Regno Unito come figlio di William Scott Balliol e Unknown Scott. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Inglis Murthuckstone. Morì nel 1320, all'età di 45 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Scott 1st Of Rankilburn and Murthuckston(은)는 1275년 영국 스코틀랜드에서 William Scott Balliol(와)과 Unknown Scott의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Inglis Murthuckstone(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1320년 45세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Scott 1st Of Rankilburn and Murthuckston родился в 1275 г. в местности Шотландия, Великобритания. Его родителей звали William Scott Balliol и Unknown Scott. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Inglis Murthuckstone. Он умер в 1320 г. в возрасте 45 лет.
